mnemonics: Connect The Dots: A graph is connected if you can trace a path from any dot (vertex) to any other dot without lifting your pen.
keyPoints: A graph is connected if there is a path between every pair of vertices.
The connectivity (k) of a graph is the minimum number of vertices whose removal results in a disconnected graph.
A graph with connectivity k is called k-connected.
commonMistakes: Assuming a graph is disconnected if it looks 'split apart' visually, without checking for a path between all vertices.
Confusing vertex connectivity with edge connectivity; they are related but distinct measures of a graph's robustness.
